job_title: Software Engineer III - Java Backend Engineer (API Marketplace)
job_nlp_title: Software Engineer III - Java Backend Engineer (API Marketplace)
taxonomy_v3: Engineering and Development, Software Engineering, Backend Software Engineer
core_skills: Software Engineering Concepts, Java 8+, Distributed Application Development, REST, Microservices
skill_match_scores: Software Engineering Concepts:1.0, Java 8+:1.0, Distributed Application Development:0.0, REST:1.0, Microservices:1.0
must_have_quals: Formal training or certification in software engineering concepts, with 3+ years of applied experience, Advanced skills in one or more programming languages, including Java 8+ for distributed application development, REST, microservices, and Spring Boot, Experience developing cloud-native applications (e.g., AWS, GCP, private cloud), Familiarity with modern SDLC practices such as Test Driven Development, Continuous Integration, Continuous Delivery, and Continuous Deployment, Experience with design systems and version control systems (e.g., Git), Hands-on experience in system design, application development, testing, and ensuring operational stability, Proficiency in all aspects of the Software Development Life Cycle
preferred_quals: Experience with build automation tools such as Maven, Experience configuring Continuous Integration pipelines in Jenkins, Knowledge of performance optimization techniques and tools
core_responsibilities: Provide regular technical guidance and direction to business partners, technical teams, contractors, and vendors, Foster a culture of diversity, inclusion, opportunity, and respect within the team, Influence peers and stakeholders to adopt innovative, leading-edge technologies, Serve as a subject matter expert in one or more technical domains, contributing to firmwide frameworks and best practices throughout the Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC), Develop secure, high-quality production code and review/debug code written by others, Drive decisions that shape product design, application functionality, and technical operations, Mentor and coach junior developers, actively participating in code reviews to promote growth and knowledge sharing, Rapidly iterate on design and implementation, even in the face of ambiguous requirements
job_summary: JPMorgan Chase is a leading financial services firm dedicated to helping individuals and businesses achieve their financial goals. The Software Engineer will be part of an agile team focused on building and enhancing technology products, leveraging technical expertise to address complex challenges and drive business impact.
